alloy_matches.reason: Same composition (Fe-20Mn-6Al-0.6C-0.15Si) and base element; prediction name includes additional description but elements identical.

process_matches.reason: Both describe as-forged condition after VIM, casting, homogenization at 1250°C/2h, forging between 1100-950°C, and furnace cooling; linked samples correspond.

sample_matches.reason: Both are initial as-forged samples with equiaxed austenite; context matches.

truth.papers.A28.title: Temperature dependence of the deformation behavior and mechanical properties of a Fe–Mn–Al–C low-density steel for cryogenic application
truth.papers.A28.paper_scope: Investigating temperature dependence of deformation behavior, mechanical properties, and impact toughness of Fe-20Mn-6Al-0.6C-0.15Si austenitic low-density steel for cryogenic applications.

prediction.papers.A28.title: Temperature dependence of the deformation behavior and mechanical properties of a Fe–Mn–Al–C low-density steel for cryogenic application
prediction.papers.A28.paper_scope: Investigation of the temperature dependence of deformation mechanisms, work hardening behavior, tensile properties, and Charpy impact toughness of Fe-20Mn-6Al-0.6C-0.15Si austenitic low-density steel at -196°C, -77°C and room temperature.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe20mn6al06c015si.alloys_no: Estimated density is 7.078 g/cm³ according to Kim et al. equation; measured density is 7.114 g/cm³.

truth.processes.proc_asforged_01.description: As-forged condition after vacuum induction melting, casting, homogenization, and forging.
truth.processes.proc_asforged_01.processes_not: Melted in 50 kg vacuum induction furnace, air cooled to RT; reheated to 1250°C for 2 h; forged between 1100°C and 950°C to 60 mm round bar, then furnace cooled to RT.

prediction.processes.proc_asforged_01.description: Vacuum induction melting and casting (50 kg ingot), air cooling to room temperature, reheating to 1250°C for 2 h homogenization, forging to 60 mm diameter round bars with starting temperature 1100°C and finishing temperature 950°C, furnace cooling to room temperature.
prediction.processes.proc_asforged_01.processes_not: As-forged initial condition used for subsequent cryogenic and room-temperature testing.

truth.processing_steps.sample_initial.processi: Forged from 1100°C to 950°C to a 60 mm diameter round bar, then furnace cooled to RT.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_initial.processi: Cooled to room temperature in furnace after forging to obtain as-forged initial microstructure.

truth.structures.sample_m196_tensile.overall_s: Deformed austenite with mechanical twins, high fraction of LAGBs, and GND accumulation
truth.structures.sample_m77_tensile.overall_st: Deformed austenite with microbands, moderate LAGB fraction, no mechanical twins
truth.structures.sample_rt_tensile.overall_str: Deformed austenite with lower LAGB fraction, planar slip features less pronounced
truth.structures.sample_m196_impact.overall_st: Fracture surface with large dimples, tear edges, quasic cleavage features; near-fracture region shows limited planar glide
truth.structures.sample_m77_impact.overall_str: Fracture surface with more ductile features, larger fibrous zone; near-fracture region shows more pronounced planar glide
truth.structures.sample_m77_impact.microstruct: transgranular fracture, planar glide features present
truth.structures.sample_rt_impact.overall_stru: Fracture surface with pronounced ductile fracture, secondary cracks present; near-fracture region shows pronounced planar glide
truth.structures.sample_rt_impact.microstructu: secondary cracks relieve stress concentration near primary crack tip

prediction.structures.sample_initial.overall_struct: Fully austenitic equiaxed grain structure, no isothermal martensite after holding at -196°C for 30 min
prediction.structures.sample_m196_tensile.overall_s: Deformed austenite with mechanical twins and high fraction of low-angle grain boundaries due to planar glide

dislocation planar glide; microbands oriented along {111} planes; low-angle grain boundaries
prediction.structures.sample_rt_tensile.overall_str: Deformed austenite with wavy glide tendency and lower low-angle grain boundary fraction

prediction.structures.sample_m196_impact.overall_st: Cross-section near fracture showing zigzag primary crack path, transgranular fracture, severe local plastic deformation, and significant local orientation gradient adjacent to primary cracks

primary cracks; transgranular fracture; local orientation gradient
severe plastic deformation near primary cracks within grain interiors
prediction.structures.sample_rt_impact.overall_stru: Cross-section near fracture showing zigzag primary crack path, transgranular fracture, secondary cracks, and stress redistribution
prediction.structures.sample_rt_impact.microstructu: grain interiors with orientation gradient near cracks
primary cracks; secondary cracks; transgranular fracture; local orientation gradient

prediction.properties.sample_m196_tensile.mechanica: Engineering stress-strain curve: stress increases from 0 to ~1320 MPa over 0-70% engineering strain; true stress at 0.5 true strain ~2100 MPa; work hardening rate shows near-linear behavior with strain after initial drop.

γ-austenite peaks; lattice parameter 0.36757 nm; no ε-martensite

Determine phase constitution of initial steel and after cryogenic holding
Initial XRD pattern shows austenite peaks γ(111), γ(200), γ(220), γ(311); no martensite formed after holding at -196°C for 30 min.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m196_ten: Mechanical twins observed along <112> on {111} plane; SAED pattern confirms twinning; dislocations present.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m77_tens: No mechanical twins; microbands oriented along {111} planes observed; dislocation planar glide features.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_rt_tensi: Observe fracture surface after tensile tests
Fracture surface observed for tensile samples; detailed morphology not explicitly described in provided text.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m196_imp: Prepare cross-section samples near impact fracture surface for EBSD
Samples polished using Gatan/685.C automated argon ion polishing system for subsequent EBSD analysis.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_m77_impa: SEM and VHX-5000 ultra-depth of field 3D microscope
Observe impact fracture surface morphology at -77°C
Larger fibrous zone than -196°C, more obvious ductile fracture characteristics in radial zone, shear lips present, compressed fracture surface.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_rt_impac: Analyze cross-section microstructure near impact fracture surface at room temperature
Zigzag primary crack path; transgranular fracture; secondary cracks present; misorientation analysis confirms transgranular fracture.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: ΔG^{γ→ε} from thermodynamic expressions in Table 1; ρ_A from lattice parameter 0.36757 nm; σ^{γ/ε}=15 mJ/m^2; equations (2)-(4).

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Predicted SFE values compared against observed deformation mechanisms: DIMT suppressed at all temperatures; mechanical twins appear only at -196°C where SFE=32 mJ/m²; planar glide dominates at 40-61 mJ/m².
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: SFE = 2ρ_A ΔG^{γ→ε} + 2σ^{γ/ε}; ρ_A = (4/√3)·(1/Nα_γ²); α_γ = 0.36757 nm; σ^{γ/ε} = 15 mJ/m²; ΔG^{γ→ε} from Table 1 thermodynamic parameters
SFE at -196°C, -77°C and RT estimated as 32, 40 and 61 mJ/m², respectively.
σ = Kε^n; lnσ = lnK + n·lnε; n1 for low strain region, n2 for high strain region
Work hardening behavior divides into two stages; n1 increases with decreasing temperature.
Analyze instrumented Charpy impact crack initiation and propagation
ΔC/C = (C - C_el)/C_el; C = ds/dF; C_el = s_el/F_el
Used to determine crack initiation load (F_in), unstable crack growth transition load (F_iu), and rebound compliance reflecting unstable crack growth rate.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Estimated density from equation (1) is 7.078 g/cm^3, measured density is 7.114 g/cm^3.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Charpy impact W_initiation values from Fig. 5b table differ from text: table gives 110, 65, 55 J for -196, -77, RT respectively; text states 112, 68, 57 J. W_stable and W_unstable from Fig. 5b: -196°C: 30, 40 J; -77°C: 180, 50 J; RT: 180, 60 J.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Linear relationships from Fig. 2c: UTS = 813.0 - 2.36*T, YS = 474.6 - 2.05*T, where T is temperature in °C.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Planar glide promotes continuous uniform deformation; lower SFE increases glide planarity but high strain rate impact at low temperature is difficult due to higher activation energy for slip systems, leading to limited planar glide and poor cryogenic toughness.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 2c provides approximate tensile properties plotted against temperature: at -200°C (corresponding to -196°C test) TE=68%, UE=47%, UTS=1300 MPa, YS=900 MPa; at -100°C (corresponding to -77°C test) TE=58%, UE=45%, UTS=1000 MPa, YS=650 MPa; at 0°C (corresponding to RT test) TE=32%, UE=28%, UTS=800 MPa, YS=400 MPa. Fig. 2c also labels fitted linear relations: UTS=813.0-2.36*T and YS=474.6-2.05*T where T is temperature in °C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Engineering stress-strain curve approximate values from Fig. 2a: at 25°C, stress rises from 0 to ~830 MPa over 0-70% strain; at -77°C, ~0-1030 MPa; at -196°C, ~0-1320 MPa. True stress-strain and work hardening rate from Fig. 2b: at 0.5 true strain, true stress is ~1000 MPa (25°C), ~1500 MPa (-77°C), ~2100 MPa (-196°C); work hardening rate dσ_true/dε_true decreases from ~2400 to ~2000 with increasing true strain.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Work hardening exponents from Fig. 8: n1 values 0.174, 0.181, 0.204 and n2=0.362. Since n1 increases with decreasing temperature, 0.204 corresponds to -196°C, 0.181 to -77°C, and 0.174 to RT. n2 represents the high strain region slope.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Charpy impact absorbed energy (W_all): approximately 327 J at RT, 277 J at -77°C, and 188 J at -196°C (from text). Fig. 5b tabulates energy components: W_initiation ~110 J (-196°C), ~65 J (-77°C), ~55 J (RT); W_stable ~30 J (-196°C), ~180 J (-77°C), ~180 J (RT); W_unstable ~40 J (-196°C), ~50 J (-77°C), ~60 J (RT). However, text states W_initiation values as 112 J (-196°C), 68 J (-77°C), and 57 J (RT), which take precedence. W_collapse values can be inferred from the difference between W_all and the sum of other components.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Impact load-deflection characteristic values from Fig. 9d approximate curves: maximum load ~32.0 kN at -200°C (approximating -196°C), ~27.0 kN at -100°C (approximating -77°C), ~24.0 kN at 0°C (approximating RT), and ~22.0 kN at 50°C. Ratio F_iu/F_m: 0.95 (-200°C), 0.85 (-100°C), 0.80 (0°C), 0.75 (50°C), indicating faster transition from stable to unstable crack propagation at lower temperatures. General yield load F_gy shows negative temperature dependence consistent with yield strength trend.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fracture surface morphology after impact (Fig. 6): at -196°C, smaller fracture surface area due to less compression, almost no fibrous zone, combination of ductile and brittle fracture with high-density large dimples and tear edges suggesting quasi-cleavage cracks, significantly flatter 3D topography. At -77°C and RT, larger fibrous zones, more obvious ductile fracture characteristics in radial zone, more severe plastic deformation (greater compression reducing surface area), and shear lips present on all samples.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Near-fracture SEM observations (Fig. 11): planar glide features observed in regions near fracture. At -196°C, only few regions experienced planar glide during impact deformation. At -77°C and RT, planar glide becomes more pronounced, which can retard crack propagation and increase toughness.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Cross-section EBSD near impact fracture (Fig. 10): primary crack propagation paths are zigzag and mostly transgranular at both -196°C and RT. Misorientation analysis along the marked line for grain A shows misorientation difference across the crack less than 15°, confirming both regions were in the same grain before cracking. Secondary cracks are present at RT but absent at -196°C; secondary cracks release stress concentration at primary crack tips and delay fracture.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: TEM observations of tensile deformed microstructure (Fig. 4): at -196°C, mechanical twins formed along <112> on {111} plane in grain near <110> zone axis, confirmed by SAED. At -77°C, no mechanical twins but uniform and dense microbands oriented along {111} planes, typical of planar glide (dynamic slip band refinement).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Tensile test conditions: strain rate 0.001 s⁻¹ (10⁻³ s⁻¹), cooling media liquid nitrogen (-196°C), dry ice-alcohol mixture (-77°C), and room temperature; specimens with parallel length 32 mm, width 5 mm, thickness 1.5 mm; entire tensile process carried out in cooling medium to avoid frictional heat.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Impact test conditions: PST752H-4 WANCE impact tester; samples 10 × 10 × 55 mm with 2 mm depth V-notch; tested at -196°C, -77°C and RT. EBSD step size for deformed tensile microstructure was 0.5 μm; for impact cross-section analyses was 0.8 μm. TEM specimens prepared by double-jet thinning using 6% perchloric acid and 94% glacial acetic acid electrolyte.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: XRD analysis of initial steel and after holding at -196°C for 30 min (Fig. 1c) confirms fully austenitic microstructure with peaks at ~45° γ(111), ~50° γ(200), ~70° γ(220), ~85° γ(311), and no isothermal martensite formation, indicating very stable austenite.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: The generalized temperature dependence of tensile elongation for austenitic steels (Fig. 7) indicates three SFE regions: Region I (high SFE, wavy glide, low elongation), Region II (moderate SFE, planar glide with mechanical twins/glide bands, high elongation), Region III (low SFE, DIMT dominant, decreased elongation). The experimental steel operates in Region II, with mechanical twinning appearing at the lower-SFE end (-196°C) and microbands at the higher-SFE end (-77°C, RT).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: The activation energy for slip systems increases with decreasing temperature, making planar glide more difficult to activate during high strain rate impact deformation despite higher glide planarity at low strain rate. This explains the poor cryogenic impact toughness despite good cryogenic tensile elongation.
